stories.title: Antonelli Makes History — Third Straight Win From Third Pole Position at Miami GP

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
KIMI ANTONELLI. 3 RACES. 3 POLES. 3 WINS.

First driver in F1 HISTORY to convert their first three pole positions into victories.

Senna did it. Schumacher did it. Now the 19-year-old from Bologna.

#F1Fanrecap #F1 #MiamiGP

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Race result — 2026 Miami GP:
🥇 Antonelli
🥈 Norris +3.3s
🥉 Piastri +27.1s
P4 Russell | P5 Verstappen | P6 Hamilton
P8 Leclerc (-20s penalty)

Antonelli: 100 pts. Russell: 80 pts. The gap is growing.

#F1Fanrecap #F1 #MiamiGP

facebook.long_form:
In a sport that has produced Senna, Schumacher, and a century of champions, what Kimi Antonelli achieved in Miami on Sunday has never been done before.

The 19-year-old Mercedes driver claimed victory at the Miami Grand Prix to complete a run of three wins from three pole positions to open the 2026 season. No driver in Formula 1 history had ever converted their first three pole positions into victories. Not Schumacher. Not Senna. Not Hamilton. Antonelli, in his debut season, just did it.

The race itself was not easy. Verstappen spun at Turn 1 in the opening laps, Leclerc incurred a penalty that shuffled the order, and Lando Norris kept the pressure on from behind. But Antonelli managed his pace and his tyres with a composure that belies his age. He crossed the line 3.264 seconds ahead of Norris, with Piastri third and Russell fourth. After the race, Antonelli was characteristically measured: "The road is still long," he said. He now leads the Drivers' Championship with 100 points — 20 ahead of teammate Russell.

The F1 calendar now pauses for three weeks before the Canadian Grand Prix on May 22-24. Antonelli heads into that break with momentum, history, and a championship that feels like it is his to lose.
facebook.image_caption: 3 poles. 3 wins. 0 drivers in history who did it before him. Kimi Antonelli is making F1 history one race at a time.

article.headline: No Driver Has Ever Done This: Antonelli Claims Third Straight Win From Third Pole, Makes F1 History in Miami

stories.title: Miami Race Chaos — Gasly Flipped Upside Down, Verstappen Spins at Turn 1, Multiple Penalties

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
PIERRE GASLY'S CAR FLIPPED UPSIDE DOWN in Miami.

Lawson had a gearbox failure — couldn't brake in time. Contact sent Gasly into the wall, inverted. Both drivers OK.

The wildest moment of the 2026 season so far.

#F1Fanrecap #F1 #MiamiGP

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Miami chaos:
→ Verstappen T1 spin (contact with Leclerc)
→ Verstappen +5s penalty (pit exit breach)
→ Leclerc +20s penalty (track limits)
→ Gasly flipped by Lawson (gearbox failure)
→ Race moved 3hrs earlier for storms

Just another Sunday in F1.

#F1Fanrecap #F1 #MiamiGP

facebook.long_form:
If you missed the 2026 Miami Grand Prix, here is the short version: a car was flipped upside down, the race leader spun on the first lap, multiple penalties were handed out after the finish, and the whole thing started three hours earlier than planned because of thunderstorms.

Pierre Gasly's Alpine was the most shocking moment of the weekend. Heading into Turn 17, Gasly moved to the outside to attempt an overtake on Liam Lawson when Lawson's Racing Bulls suffered a sudden gearbox failure. Lawson was unable to brake in time and made contact with the rear of Gasly's car, sending the Frenchman's Alpine flipping upside down into the barrier. Both drivers walked away unharmed. The stewards investigated but did not penalize Lawson — his gearbox failure was deemed a mechanical failure beyond his control.

Meanwhile, at the very start of the race, Verstappen and Leclerc made contact going into Turn 1, causing the Red Bull driver to spin and duck into the pit lane for an early stop under the Safety Car. Verstappen was then handed a five-second time penalty for crossing the pit exit white line during that stop. Leclerc received a 20-second penalty after the race for repeatedly leaving the track without justifiable reason — a penalty that dropped him from sixth to eighth.

The race had been moved from its original 3 p.m. local start to 6 p.m. because of a thunderstorm threat. Even with the earlier start, nothing could contain the chaos that followed.
facebook.image_caption: Gasly's car upside down. Verstappen spinning Turn 1. Leclerc penalized 20 seconds. Just another Sunday at the Miami GP — where F1's drama never disappoints.

article.headline: Gasly Upside Down, Verstappen Spinning, Leclerc Penalized: Miami Was F1 at Its Most Chaotic

stories.title: Norris Claims Sprint Win — McLaren's Best Weekend Package Shows Title Fight Is On

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
LANDO NORRIS wins the Miami Sprint — his first victory of 2026.

Piastri P2. McLaren 1-2. Antonelli penalized from P4 to P6 for track limits.

The papaya cars are showing up at the right time. #F1Fanrecap #F1 #MiamiGP

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
McLaren's Miami weekend: Sprint win + P2 + P3 in the main race.

Norris is 47 points behind Antonelli. That gap WAS 53 before Miami.

If the papaya momentum continues, this championship fight won't be over for long.

#F1Fanrecap #F1 #McLaren

facebook.long_form:
Lando Norris has been close to the top of the 2026 grid in every race — and on Saturday afternoon in Miami, he finally got the result to prove it.

Norris took the lead at the start of the Miami Sprint and never looked back, crossing the line first for McLaren's — and his own — first win of the 2026 season. Teammate Oscar Piastri followed him home in second for a McLaren 1-2 finish. Ferrari's Charles Leclerc was third, while Kimi Antonelli — who had qualified second and was running fourth — was penalized five seconds for repeatedly exceeding track limits and dropped to sixth.

It was a revealing sprint. Norris was clinical and measured. Piastri continued the form that has made McLaren one of the most consistently impressive outfits in the paddock. The Saturday result was then backed up on Sunday when both Norris and Piastri stood on the main race podium, finishing second and third behind Antonelli.

Norris trails Antonelli by 47 points after Miami. That gap was larger before. The direction of travel for McLaren is clear — the team is getting faster at precisely the moment the season needs them to be competitive. Canada in three weeks will be a critical test of whether the papaya cars can get even closer.
facebook.image_caption: LANDO NORRIS wins the Miami Sprint — McLaren's first victory of 2026. P2 Piastri. The papaya cars are coming. The championship fight is very much on.

article.headline: Norris Finally Wins in 2026 — Miami Sprint Shows McLaren Is Ready to Challenge for the Title

stories.title: Championship Update — Antonelli 100 Points, Russell 80, Mercedes Leads; Norris Climbs

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
2026 F1 Drivers' Championship — Round 4 of 24:

1. Antonelli — 100 pts
2. Russell — 80 pts
3. Leclerc
4. Norris
5. Hamilton

Mercedes Constructors: 180 pts and dominant.

"The road is still long." — Antonelli

#F1Fanrecap #F1 #F1Standings

facebook.long_form:
Four races into the 2026 Formula 1 season, the story is the same — but the margins are growing.

Kimi Antonelli leads the Drivers' Championship with 100 points after his Miami victory. Teammate George Russell sits second with 80, having been one of the most consistent performers of the opening rounds even when the spotlight has been on Antonelli. The gap is now 20 points and growing.

Charles Leclerc holds third in the championship for Ferrari, though the 20-second Miami penalty stings — it cost him what would have been a much stronger haul of points. Lando Norris has climbed above Lewis Hamilton into fourth, a reflection of McLaren's improving pace. Hamilton, in his first Ferrari campaign, has been solid but not spectacular at five on the points table.

For the Constructors, Mercedes leads with 180 points, a position built on Antonelli's three wins and Russell's steady contributions. The remaining teams are competing for the scraps behind the Silver Arrows.

The next opportunity for anyone to challenge Antonelli's dominance comes at the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve in Montreal — the Canadian Grand Prix, May 22-24. Three weeks from now.
facebook.image_caption: Antonelli: 100 pts. Russell: 80 pts. Mercedes: 180 pts in the Constructors. Four races in, the championship picture is becoming very clear — and very silver.

article.headline: F1 2026 Championship Standings After Miami: Antonelli Has 100 Points and a 20-Point Lead — But He Says the Road Is Still Long

stories.title: Canada Preview — What to Watch as F1's Perfect Start Takes His Act to Montreal

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
F1 takes a 3-week break. Next up: Canadian GP — May 22-24 in Montreal.

Circuit Gilles Villeneuve. Wall of Champions. Chaos is always possible.

Can Antonelli make it 4 in a row? Or does someone finally stop the Mercedes machine?

#F1Fanrecap #F1 #CanadaGP

facebook.long_form:
The 2026 Formula 1 season takes a three-week breather after one of its most chaotic and historic weekends. When it resumes, it will do so in one of motorsport's most beloved venues.

The Canadian Grand Prix — Round 5 of the 2026 season — takes place May 22-24 at the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve on the man-made Notre-Dame island in the heart of Montreal. The circuit is famous for its unpredictability: the hairpin at the Senna curve, the notorious Wall of Champions at the final chicane that has claimed pole-sitters and world champions alike, and weather patterns that can shift dramatically through a race weekend.

All the major narratives coming out of Miami carry into Canada. Can Antonelli make it four wins from four races — a feat that would truly make him the overwhelming favorite for the championship? Can McLaren's Norris and Piastri convert their Miami form into a race victory? Will Red Bull and Verstappen finally find the answers to the RB22's balance issues? And will Leclerc, stung by his Miami penalty drama, come out swinging on a circuit that could suit Ferrari?

Three weeks of factory work from every team. Three weeks for Antonelli to enjoy the fact that he has just made history. Then Montreal.
facebook.image_caption: After Miami chaos, F1 takes 3 weeks off. Next stop: Canadian GP — May 22-24 at Circuit Gilles Villeneuve. Can anyone stop Antonelli's perfect start?

article.headline: Three Weeks, One Question: Can Anyone Stop Antonelli's Perfect Season at the Canadian Grand Prix?
